title: chamaesaracha coronopus
intro: Chamaesaracha coronopus, also known as Five-horned Chamaesaracha or Desert Five-horn, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the nightshade family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Chamaesaracha coronopus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health conditions.
